# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.
# Category: container_security

FROM node:14

# Update packages lists and upgrade the system
RUN apt-get update && apt-get upgrade -y

# Install build-essential package to include essential utilities like make
RUN apt-get install -y build-essential

# Install other necessary dependencies
RUN apt-get install -y curl wget gnupg lsb-release

# Set up the Node.js repository for the debian packages and add key
RUN curl -sL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_14.x | bash -

# Install Node.js version 14.x
RUN apt-get install -y nodejs

# Cache the dependencies for faster build times in production
RUN npm cache clean --force && echo "//registry.npmjs.org/" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/npm.list
RUN apt-key adv --keyserver hkp://keys.openpgp.org --recv 825DC07F
RUN apt-get update -y && apt-get install -y npm
